english: rain was falling Dzl., Mil.; čar zim-zím dál-gyis báb-pa (char zim zim dal gyis bab pa) Mil., id.; zim-zím (zim zim) or zíṅ-zíṅ (zing zing) fine, hair-shaped, capillary, e.g. the leaves of some plants.
(γzír-ba?) (gzir ba?), gen. *zir táṅ-če* (zir tang che) W., to aim, zír-po (zir po), zír-čan (zir can) a good aimer, marksman W.; zír-sa (zir sa) aim, dispart, *ne-zír* (ne zir) sight (of a gun) W.
*zir-mo gyúṅ-če* (zir mo gyung che) W. to slide down a snow-hill on the coat spread under, a winter-diversion of children.

1. (Cs. zil-ma (zil ma)), brightness, splendour, brilliancy, glory, rǰe-btsún-gyi ṭúgs-rǰei zil ma bzód-par (rje btsun gyi thugs rje'i zil ma bzod par) not being able to bear the brightness of his Reverence's grace, (the adversary fell down the mountain) Mil.; zil-čán (zil can) brilliant, resplendent; zil-gyís nón-pa (zil gyis non pa) to overcome, vanquish, kói zil-gyis nón-te (kho'i zil gyis non te) overpowered by him Pth.; zil-bar ʽgró-ba (zil bar 'gro ba) to increase, multiply, spread Sch. — 2. in botany: stoṅ-zíl (stong zil), Corydalis meïfolia; γser-zíl (gser zil), dṅul-zíl? (dngul zil?) S.g.
dew, zil-pa kṛom-mé (zil pa khrom me) a sparkling dew-drop Pth.; zil-dkár (zil dkar) hoar-frost Sch.; zil-mṅar (zil mngar) Cs. = mdúd-rtsi (mdud rtsi) nectar.

1. also γzug (gzug), pain, torment, physical and mental; distemper, illness, complaint, esp. W. *zúg rag* (zug rag) I feel a pain, I am ill, *gó-la zug rág-ga* (go la zug rag ga) have you the head-ache? *zug čo dug* (zug co dug) he is ill, he is suffering from pain; *só-zug* (so zug) toothache; zúg-rṅu (zug rngu), zug-γzér (zug gzer), resp. snyúṅ (snyung) or snyúṅ-zug (snyung zug), B. and col. = zug (zug), mya-ṅán-gyi zúg-rṅus sdúg-bsṅal-žiṅ (mya ngan gyi zug rngus sdug bsngal zhing) weighed down by the grief of misery, nyon-móṅs-kyi zúg-rṅu (nyon mongs kyi zug rngu) Mil., of the like import. — 2. also γzug (gzug), the principal or main pieces in cutting up an animal, quarters, zug-tu ʽḍrál-ba (zug tu 'dral ba) to cut into such pieces Mil.; 1 zug (zug) = 3 lhu (lhu) = 6 dum (dum) = 12 rgya-ri (rgya ri). — 3. v. tsug (tsug).
I. vb. 1. v. ʽdzúgs-pa ('dzugs pa). — 2. to bark Dzl. II. sbst. building, erection, *zúg-pa gyáb-pa* (zug pa gyab pa) Ts. to build (cf. ʽdzúgs-pa ('dzugs pa) 3).

1. earlier literat. and W. a pair, couple, zúṅ-du ma mčis (zung du ma mchis) not occurring in pairs Wdn.; *čá-bu zuṅ čig* (cha bu zung cig), Ld. a pair of pendants (for the ears); nyí-zlá zuṅ γčig btsón-du bzuṅ (nyi zla zung gcig btson du bzung) sun and moon are both shut up (covered by clouds) Mil.; zuṅ-mčóg (zung mchog) the model-pair, the two principal disciples of Buddha, Sariibu and Maudgalgyibu, Köpp. I, 101; zuṅ-ldán (zung ldan) agreeing in sound, rhyming Cs.; zuṅ-ʽbrél (zung 'brel) connection, junction, union, zuṅ-ʽbrel ʽdód-na (zung 'brel 'dod na) if one wishes both things to be united Glr.; zuṅ-brél-du (zung brel du) one after the other, or one with the other Pth.; zuṅ sdébs-pa (zung sdebs pa) to join, connect, unite Mil.; zuṅ-yá (zung ya) one half of a pair, a single one, e.g. shoe etc. Cs. — 2. a single, separate piece C. and sometimes in later literat.; k̇a-dród zuṅ čig (kha drod zung cig) a bit or mouthful of food Thgy.; tsar re zuṅ re bltás-pas (tsar re zung re bltas pas) when he had seen a single piece but once, (he knew it immediately) Tar. — 3. symb. num.: 2; zuṅ-pyógs (zung phyogs) id. — zuṅ-ʽjúg (zung 'jug) a technical term of practical mysticism, the forcing the mind (sems (sems)) into the principal artery, in order to prevent distraction (of mind) (!) Mil. (v. gtúm-mo (gtum mo)).
n. of a royal castle Glr.
inst. of bsúbs-pa (bsubs pa), pf. of sub-pa (sub pa) Glr.
1. v. ʽdzúm-pa ('dzum pa). — 2. W. for bzúṅ-ba (bzung ba), v. ʽdzín-pa ('dzin pa); hence zum-káb (zum khab) pin, brooch.

1. edge, gad-zúr (gad zur) edge of a steep river-bank or precipice consisting of conglomerate Cs.; ču-zúr (chu zur) edge of the water, border, brink, bank, ču-zúr-pa (chu zur pa) one that lives on the bank of a river; zúr-na (zur na) at the border (of the place where one happens to be) Mil. *žiṅ-zúr-ne lam yod* (zhing zur ne lam yod) W. the road leads along the field; board, of a ship. — 2. edge, corner, ká-ba zur-brgyád-pa (ka ba zur brgyad pa)
